title: CSA CCM v4 to AWS Security Services Cross-Reference
description: Maps CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4 domains to AWS security services and features that implement or support each domain's control objectives. Use to identify which AWS services satisfy CCM requirements for cloud security assessments.
verificationNote: AWS service mappings are based on publicly documented service capabilities as of 2025. AWS services evolve frequently -- verify current capabilities against AWS documentation. Not endorsed by CSA or AWS.

awsServices.description: Automated evidence collection for cloud audits. Pre-built frameworks for SOC 2, PCI DSS, ISO 27001. Maps directly to A&A-01 (audit planning) and A&A-02 (independent assessments).
Continuous configuration compliance monitoring. Records configuration changes and evaluates against compliance rules. Supports A&A-03 (risk-based planning).
Access to AWS compliance reports (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS AOC). Supports A&A-05 (information system regulatory mapping).
Automated vulnerability assessment for EC2, Lambda, and container images. Supports AIS-02 (application security testing).
Web Application Firewall for CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway. OWASP Top 10 managed rules. Implements AIS-01 (application security) for web applications.
Managed API gateway with throttling, authentication, request validation. Supports AIS-04 (secure application design) for API security.
CI/CD pipeline with security testing integration. Supports AIS-02 (security testing in SDLC).
Centralised backup service across AWS services. Cross-region and cross-account backup. Supports BCR-01 and BCR-04 (backup and recovery).
Continuous replication and automated recovery for DR. Supports BCR-02 (business continuity testing).
Immutable object storage (WORM). Protects backups from ransomware. Supports BCR-04 (backup integrity).
AWS Availability Zones and Regions provide infrastructure redundancy. Supports BCR-01 (business continuity planning).
Continuous configuration recording and compliance evaluation. Detects configuration drift. Implements CCC-03 (configuration management).
Infrastructure as Code for controlled, repeatable deployments. Implements CCC-01 (change management) through code-based changes.
Configuration management, patching, and inventory. State Manager for desired state enforcement. Supports CCC-03.
Approved cloud service catalogue with governance constraints. Supports CCC-02 (quality testing) through pre-approved templates.
Customer-managed encryption key management. CMK creation, rotation, access policies. Implements EKM-01 through EKM-04.
Dedicated HSM for cryptographic key storage. FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated. Implements EKM-02 for highest assurance key management.
TLS certificate provisioning and renewal. Implements DSP-02 (encryption in transit).
Service-level encryption at rest. Implements DSP-01 (encryption at rest) when combined with KMS CMEK.
ML-powered sensitive data discovery and classification for S3. Detects PII, financial data, credentials. Implements DSP-01 (data classification).
Fine-grained data access control for data lakes. Column-level and row-level security. Supports DSP-05 (data access control).
Account-level and bucket-level public access prevention. Implements DSP-04 (data access restriction).
Organisation-level region restriction and service restriction. Implements DSP-06 (data residency) by preventing resource creation in non-approved regions.
Automated data lifecycle management (retention, archival, deletion). Implements DSP-17 (data retention and deletion).

Datacenter physical security is an AWS responsibility. Customers verify via SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 reports covering physical access controls, environmental protections, and equipment security. Maps to DCS-01 through DCS-09.
AWS datacenters comply with SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001, ISO 27017, PCI DSS, and FedRAMP — all of which include physical security audit scope.
Multi-account governance with SCPs, consolidated billing, and centralised policy management. Implements GRC-01 (governance programme) for cloud.
Automated multi-account setup with guardrails and compliance baseline. Implements GRC-01 and GRC-03 (policy enforcement).
Centralised security posture dashboard aggregating findings from multiple AWS security services. Supports GRC-02 (risk management).
Core identity and access management. Users, roles, policies, groups. Implements IAM-01 (access control policy) and IAM-04 (provisioning).
Centralised SSO and MFA for multi-account access. Implements IAM-02 (strong authentication) and IAM-03 (MFA).
Automated analysis of IAM policies for external and unused access. Implements IAM-06 (access reviews) and IAM-09 (least privilege).
Temporary credential issuance. Eliminates long-lived API keys. Implements IAM-10 (credential management).
API call logging for all IAM operations. Supports IAM-12 (access logging) and LOG-01.
Virtual private network with subnets, security groups, NACLs, and VPC endpoints. Implements IVS-01 (network security) and IVS-06 (segmentation).
DDoS protection for AWS resources. Shield Advanced provides enhanced protection with cost protection. Implements IVS-04.
Managed network firewall for VPC with stateful inspection and IDS/IPS. Implements IVS-01 and IVS-04.
Intelligent threat detection using ML on VPC flow logs, DNS logs, and CloudTrail. Implements IVS-09 (threat detection).
Private connectivity to AWS services without internet transit. Implements IVS-06 (network segmentation) for service access.
Management and data event logging across all AWS services and regions. Implements LOG-01 (logging policy) and LOG-03 (security monitoring).
Metrics, logs, alarms, and dashboards. Implements LOG-03 (monitoring) and LOG-13 (alerting).
Centralised security data lake in OCSF format. Implements LOG-02 (log centralisation) and LOG-05 (log retention).
Network traffic logging for VPC subnets and interfaces. Implements LOG-01 for network-level visibility.

awsServices.service: S3 Object Lock for log immutability
awsServices.description: Write-once-read-many protection for audit logs. Implements LOG-05 (log integrity and retention).

mappings.ccmDomainName: Security Incident Management, E-Discovery, & Cloud Forensics

awsServices.description: Centralised security finding aggregation and automated response. Supports SEF-01 (incident management) through finding correlation.
Graph-based security investigation and analysis. Supports SEF-02 (incident response) through automated investigation.
Incident response management with runbooks. Implements SEF-02 (incident response plan) and SEF-04 (incident response testing).

awsServices.service: EBS Snapshots / S3 for forensic preservation
awsServices.description: Volume snapshots and object storage for forensic evidence preservation. Supports cloud forensics capability.
Access to AWS compliance documents (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS). Supports STA-05 (third-party audit) for AWS vendor assessment.
Vetted third-party software marketplace. Supports STA-01 (vendor assessment) through AWS vendor vetting.
Private container registry with image scanning. Supports STA-06 (supply chain security) for container images.
Managed artefact repository. Supports STA-06 by providing controlled package management.
Automated vulnerability assessment for EC2, Lambda, ECR. Implements TVM-01 (vulnerability management) and TVM-02 (vulnerability scanning).
ML-based threat detection across CloudTrail, VPC Flow, DNS, S3, EKS, and Lambda. Implements TVM-06 (threat intelligence).
Automated OS and application patching. Implements TVM-01 (vulnerability remediation).
Container image vulnerability scanning. Implements TVM-04 (container security).

awsServices.service: AWS IAM Identity Center + Conditional Access
awsServices.description: Device trust evaluation for cloud access. Supports UEM controls through device-based access policies.
Managed virtual desktop infrastructure. Supports UEM by providing controlled cloud endpoint environments.
